paras.text: Class presentation ka din tha. Teacher ne achanak {{B}} ka naam bol diya stage pe bolne ke liye, bina poochhe.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (class ke baad, halke gusse me) Tumne teacher ko mera naam kyu diya presentation ke liye? Tumhe pata hai mujhe stage pe bolna kitna mushkil lagta hai!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (ghabra ke) Sorry yaar, mujhe laga tumhe pasand aayega, tum hamesha itni confident lagti ho baaton me...

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Baaton me aur stage pe bolne me bahut fark hai. If you had asked me first, I would not have panicked in front of everyone.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sar jhukate hue) Sach me sorry {{B}}. Maine sirf achha karne ki koshish ki thi, galat soch liya.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (thodi der chup rehke, phir narmi se) Pata hai mujhe tumne bura nahi socha, but agli baar please pehle poochh lena, theek hai?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Promise. Waise... agar main tumse pehle poochhta, tum haan bolti kya?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (halki si muskurahat, but aankhon me thodi doori) Shayad. Pata nahi. Abhi chalte hain, class late ho rahi hai.
Interview hall ke bahar, us din ki yaad aate hi uska chehra thoda udaas ho gaya — kaash us din kuch aur hota...

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'If I would have known about the meeting, I would have come.'
quiz.answer: If I had known about the meeting, I would have come.
quiz.explain: 'If' clause me 'would have' nahi aata — hamesha 'had + V3' aata hai Type 3 Conditional me; 'would have' sirf main clause me.

quiz.q: Fill (past regret): If she ___ (study) harder, she ___ (pass) the exam last year.
quiz.explain: Past ki ek unreal situation ka pachtawa — If + had + V3, Subject + would have + V3.

quiz.q: Which sentence correctly expresses a past regret — a) If you had told me, I would have helped. b) If you told me, I will help.
quiz.answer: a) If you had told me, I would have helped.
quiz.explain: Ye Type 3 Conditional hai jo past ki ek unreal situation aur uska regret dikhata hai; option b) galat structure hai.
